title: The agent-ready web: Simplify user actions with WebMCP — Tara Agyemang, Google
summary: WebMCP is a proposed web standard from Google Chrome that enables AI agents to interact with websites through structured tools instead of DOM parsing and pixel-coordinate clicking. It provides both declarative HTML-based and imperative JavaScript APIs for defining agent-callable actions on web pages.
writeup: This talk introduces WebMCP, a web standard proposed by Google Chrome that lets AI agents interact with websites through structured, agent-callable tools rather than parsing the DOM and clicking by pixel coordinates. It covers two ways to define those actions: a declarative HTML-based approach and an imperative JavaScript API. The shift matters because structured tools give agents a more reliable interface to web pages than screen-reading and coordinate-based clicking. The key takeaway is that sites can expose explicit actions for agents to call, reducing reliance on brittle DOM and pixel-level interaction.
